name: Wv National Guard
description_short: Federal credit union serving West Virginia National Guard members and their families with $51.17M in assets, offering checking, savings, and lending products since 1959.
description_long:
WV National Guard Federal Credit Union is a federally-chartered credit union headquartered in Charleston, West Virginia, that has operated for 67 years since its founding in 1959. It serves approximately 1,840 members with a primary membership focus on the West Virginia National Guard and related military personnel, representing a multiple common bond membership model. The institution maintains a single branch location at 1512 Coonskin Drive in Charleston and employs 5 full and part-time staff members.

The credit union offers a range of traditional banking and financial services including checking accounts, savings accounts, ATM/Debit Card programs, and credit and lending products. Their loan portfolio totals approximately $27 million, comprising real estate loans ($6.79M), unsecured loans ($1.73M), and other loans ($18.48M). They also provide services such as low-cost wire transfers and maintain NCUSIF deposit insurance protection for member accounts.

As a federally-chartered credit union with a specialized military membership base, WV National Guard FCU distinguishes itself through its targeted focus on serving the National Guard community rather than the general public. The institution maintains a strong capital position with $7.16M in equity against $51.17M in total assets, demonstrating financial stability. Their most recent audit was completed on October 1, 2024, confirming regulatory compliance.

The credit union's small size and single-branch model limits convenience for members outside the Charleston area, and their asset base is modest compared to larger regional institutions. However, for eligible members seeking personalized service and products tailored to military personnel, the institution provides a legitimate alternative to commercial banks. The organization's 67-year operational history and stable financial metrics suggest reliable basic banking services for its target membership.
pros: Federally chartered with NCUSIF insurance protection for member deposits
Strong equity position of $7.16M indicates financial stability and solvency
Specialized focus on West Virginia National Guard members and military personnel
Low-cost wire transfer services explicitly advertised
Diverse loan portfolio including real estate, unsecured, and other loan products
Established 67-year history demonstrating operational longevity since 1959
Recent audit completion (October 1, 2024) confirms regulatory compliance
cons: Single branch location limits physical access for members outside Charleston area
Small membership base of only 1,840 members may limit service expansion or product offerings
Limited online presence—minimal website information about digital banking capabilities
Asset base of $51.17M is relatively small, potentially limiting loan amounts or investment options
Only 5 full and part-time employees may restrict service availability and responsiveness
best_for: West Virginia National Guard members and their families eligible for membership
Military personnel seeking personalized, community-focused banking services
Charleston, WV residents needing basic checking, savings, and lending products
Members prioritizing credit union structure and member-owned institution benefits
diagnosis: WV National Guard Federal Credit Union is best suited for eligible West Virginia National Guard members and military-connected individuals in the Charleston area seeking basic banking services from a federally-insured, member-owned institution. The primary limitation is geographic—with only one branch location and no clear digital banking infrastructure described, members outside Charleston will face inconvenience accessing services.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
